_**User**_

Something I would like to see - but tell me if it's on a later phase first; 

When the reception officer create the case; it just create the case; but for me he should not be able to consult the case; 
Only the population officer should be able to. 

THe police clerck neither shouldn't be able to see the open case. 

Also I want an overview of who did what - so for example I want to see that the reception officer create the case; then when a population open the case (page case) he assign the case to himself

If we have two population officer, once a case is locked to someone, another officer can't edit anything to it unless the previous officer release the lock. 

An admin role later one will be able to release a lock; or/and we could send a "Release lock notifications" from a colleague to another one. 



---

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user wants a read-only view when another officer holds the case lock.

This is new work, not part of the current roadmap. Existing foundations include roles, AssignedOfficerId metadata, and audit timelines. It should be inserted as a new mini-phase such as Phase 7.1 or 8.1, separate from the ongoing certificate work. The plan will answer the user's question, review current capabilities, and propose an architecture.

Implementation steps, deferred features, and tests are now being mapped out. Domain schema changes are being defined: `AssignedOfficerId` will remain nullable until a population officer claims the case, while new `LockedByOfficerId` and `LockedAt` fields will track active locks. Audit action semantics are also being refined to distinguish case creation from assignment.

The user expects assignment and locking to occur simultaneously when a population officer opens a case. This suggests `AssignedOfficerId` and `LockedByOfficerId` may initially share the same value. However, the two concepts could diverge if an administrator reassigns a case without granting a fresh lock. The user explicitly requires that a case remain exclusively editable by its current lock holder until that holder releases it.

Releasing a lock will clear the active lock holder while preserving the assigned officer for historical tracking. Another population officer can then claim and re-lock the case. Alternatively, release could fully reset the assignment and return the case to the queue.

The intended flow is now clearer. A reception officer creates a case with no assigned or locked officer. When a population officer opens it, the system assigns the case to that officer and acquires the exclusive lock. Another population officer attempting to open the same case sees it as read-only. Once the original holder releases the lock, the next officer can claim and re-lock the case. Whether the assigned officer field persists after release is still being weighed.

A central authorization guard will sit in both API handlers and the UI to enforce role boundaries. Reception officers will see only a new-case entry point, with case lists and review dashboards hidden. Police clerks will lose access to registration cases and the review dashboard entirely. The assigned officer field will persist across lock releases and only overwrite when another population officer claims the case.
</details></think>

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="create_plan"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **create_plan**</summary>

Parameters:

````json
{
  "name": "Role boundaries and locking",
  "overview": "This is **not** on a later roadmap phase. Phases 8–11 cover certificates, exceptions, and infrastructure — not role-based case visibility or officer locking. The codebase has roles and partial audit (decisions only), but no enforcement. This plan adds reception/police boundaries, population case claiming with exclusive edit locks, expanded audit timeline, and defers admin unlock and colleague notifications.",
  "plan": "# Role boundaries, case locking, and activity timeline\n\n## Is this a later phase?\n\n**No.** Nothing in [docs/ROADMAP.md](docs/ROADMAP.md) Phases 8–11 describes:\n- Reception-only create (no consult)\n- Police exclusion from open cases\n- Officer edit locks\n- Full “who did what” activity log\n\nWhat **does** exist today (partial foundation):\n\n| Piece | Status |\n|-------|--------|\n| Three demo roles (`ReceptionOfficer`, `PopulationOfficer`, `PoliceClerk`) | UI switcher only — no route/API enforcement |\n| `AssignedOfficerId` on [RegistrationCase.cs](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Registration/RegistrationCase.cs) | Set at open to current officer; **not enforced** |\n| `CaseAuditEntry` + timeline UI | Records **decisions + certificates only**; enum defines `CaseOpened`, intake, police actions but they are **never recorded** |\n| Police portal | Separate nav item; police can still browse full case list and open case detail |\n\nThis fits best as a **new mini-phase** (e.g. **Phase 8.1 — Role boundaries \u0026 case locking**) alongside or right after the in-progress Phase 8 certificates work.\n\n---\n\n## Target behavior\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    subgraph reception [ReceptionOfficer]\n        Create[\"OpenRegistrationCase\"]\n        Create --\u003e Confirm[\"Success confirmation only\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph population [PopulationOfficer]\n        List[\"List cases + review dashboard\"]\n        Open[\"Open case detail\"]\n        Open --\u003e|\"unassigned or lock released\"| Claim[\"Assign + lock to self\"]\n        Open --\u003e|\"locked to self\"| Edit[\"Full edit access\"]\n        Open --\u003e|\"locked to other\"| ReadOnly[\"Read-only view + banner\"]\n        Release[\"Release lock button\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph police [PoliceClerk]\n        PoliceQueue[\"Police verifications queue only\"]\n        Record[\"RecordPoliceResult inline\"]\n    end\n\n    Create -.-\u003e|\"no list/detail access\"| List\n    PoliceQueue --\u003e Record\n```\n\n### Role matrix (after implementation)\n\n| Action | Reception | Population | Police |\n|--------|-----------|------------|--------|\n| Create case | Yes | Yes | No |\n| List / view open cases | **No** | Yes | **No** |\n| Edit intake / send to police / decide | No | Yes, if **lock holder** | No |\n| Police verification queue | No | No | Yes |\n| Record police result | No | No | Yes |\n| View case audit timeline | No | Yes (on case detail) | No (unless we add a minimal police-only view later) |\n\n**Your choice confirmed:** when Officer B opens a case locked to Officer A → **read-only detail** with banner (not access denied).\n\n**Deferred (as you suggested):** Admin role, admin force-unlock, colleague “release lock” notifications.\n\n---\n\n## Domain model changes\n\nExtend [RegistrationCase.cs](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Registration/RegistrationCase.cs):\n\n- **`AssignedOfficerId`** → nullable (`OfficerId?`) — unassigned until a population officer claims the case\n- **`LockedByOfficerId`** → nullable — exclusive edit lock\n- **`LockedAt`** → nullable timestamp\n\nNew domain methods:\n\n- `Claim(OfficerId officer, DateTimeOffset at)` — sets `AssignedOfficerId`, `LockedByOfficerId`, `LockedAt` when case is unassigned or unlocked\n- `ReleaseLock(OfficerId officer, DateTimeOffset at)` — clears lock fields; only the current lock holder may release\n- `EnsureEditableBy(OfficerId officer, string operation)` — throws if locked to someone else (used by all mutating handlers)\n\nUpdate `RegistrationCase.Open(...)`:\n\n- Reception path: **no assignee** (`AssignedOfficerId = null`)\n- Remove auto-assign from [OpenRegistrationCaseHandler.cs](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/OpenRegistrationCase/OpenRegistrationCaseHandler.cs) when role is reception; population may still open cases directly in dev/demo if needed\n\n**Migration:** new nullable columns + backfill existing rows (`assigned_officer_id` → also set `locked_by_officer_id` for in-progress cases, or clear locks and leave assignee as metadata only — pick one consistent rule in migration).\n\n---\n\n## New audit actions\n\nAdd to [CaseAuditAction.cs](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Registration/CaseAuditAction.cs):\n\n- `CaseAssigned` — population officer claimed the case\n- `CaseLockReleased` — officer released the lock\n\nRecord via existing [CaseAuditRecorder.cs](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/CaseAuditRecorder.cs):\n\n| Event | When |\n|-------|------|\n| `CaseOpened` | `OpenRegistrationCaseHandler` (reception or population) |\n| `CaseAssigned` | `ClaimRegistrationCaseHandler` |\n| `CaseLockReleased` | `ReleaseCaseLockHandler` |\n| `PoliceVerificationRequested` | existing send-to-police handler |\n| `PoliceResultRecorded` | existing police result handler |\n\nExpand [RegistrationCaseDetail.razor](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/Pages/RegistrationCaseDetail.razor) `FormatAuditAction` labels for all enum values so the timeline reads naturally (“Case opened by reception”, “Case assigned to officer”, etc.).\n\nOptional follow-up (not required for your stated goal): wire intake handlers to record `IdentityRecorded`, `AddressDeclared`, etc.\n\n---\n\n## New application slices\n\n| Slice | Purpose |\n|-------|---------|\n| `ClaimRegistrationCase` | Called when population officer opens case detail; assigns + locks if eligible |\n| `ReleaseCaseLock` | Lock holder releases; audit + DTO refresh |\n\nExpose on [RegistrationEndpoints.cs](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/RegistrationEndpoints.cs):\n\n- `POST /api/registration/cases/{id}/claim`\n- `POST /api/registration/cases/{id}/release-lock`\n\nExtend `RegistrationCaseDetailDto` with:\n\n- `AssignedOfficerId`, `LockedByOfficerId`, `LockedAt`\n- `CanEdit` (computed: population + (unlocked or locked to me))\n- `IsReadOnlyDueToLock` (locked to another officer)\n\n---\n\n## Authorization layer\n\nIntroduce a small scoped service, e.g. `RegistrationCaseAuthorization` in `Web/Features/Registration/`:\n\n```csharp\nbool CanCreateCase(OfficerRole role);\nbool CanListCases(OfficerRole role);\nbool CanViewCase(OfficerRole role, RegistrationCase case);\nbool CanEditCase(OfficerRole role, RegistrationCase case, OfficerId currentOfficerId);\nbool CanRecordPoliceResult(OfficerRole role);\n```\n\nApply consistently:\n\n1. **Handlers** — throw `UnauthorizedAccessException` or domain exception at top of `Handle`\n2. **UI** — nav filtering, hide buttons, read-only sections\n\nKey files to gate:\n\n- [ListRegistrationCasesHandler.cs](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/ListRegistrationCases/ListRegistrationCasesHandler.cs)\n- [GetRegistrationCaseHandler.cs](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/GetRegistrationCase/GetRegistrationCaseHandler.cs)\n- [OpenRegistrationCaseHandler.cs](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/OpenRegistrationCase/OpenRegistrationCaseHandler.cs)\n- All mutating intake/decision handlers — add `EnsureEditableBy` after load\n- [RecordPoliceResultHandler.cs](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/RecordPoliceResult/RecordPoliceResultHandler.cs) — police role only\n\n---\n\n## UI changes\n\n### Reception ([MainLayout.razor](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Components/Layout/MainLayout.razor))\n\n- Nav for `ReceptionOfficer`: **Home + New case only** (hide Review dashboard, Registration cases, Police, Outbound notifications)\n- New page or dialog-only flow: `/registration/new-case` — create case, show success snackbar/dialog with short case reference, **no navigation to detail**\n- Block direct URL access to `/registration/cases` and `/registration/cases/{id}` (redirect or forbidden page)\n\n### Population\n\n- [RegistrationCaseList.razor](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/Pages/RegistrationCaseList.razor): show assignee/lock indicator column (“Unassigned”, “Marie Dupont”, “Locked — Luc”)\n- [RegistrationCaseDetail.razor](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/Pages/RegistrationCaseDetail.razor):\n  - On load: call `ClaimRegistrationCase` when population + eligible\n  - Banner when read-only due to lock (shows lock holder name)\n  - “Release lock” button when current officer holds the lock\n  - `CanEditIntakeData` becomes `CanEditIntakeData \u0026\u0026 _case.CanEdit`\n\n### Police\n\n- Nav: **Police verifications only** (+ Home)\n- [PoliceVerificationList.razor](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/Pages/PoliceVerificationList.razor): remove `MudLink` to case detail (lines 58–59); record results inline only\n- Block `/registration/cases*` routes for police role\n\n### Review dashboard\n\n- [GetReviewDashboardHandler.cs](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/GetReviewDashboard/GetReviewDashboardHandler.cs): “My open cases” = assigned **and locked to** current officer (or assigned with active lock)\n\n---\n\n## Tests\n\nAdd integration tests in `tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration/`:\n\n1. Reception can open case; cannot list/get case\n2. Population claims unassigned case → assignee + lock set; audit has `CaseOpened` + `CaseAssigned`\n3. Second population officer gets case detail with `CanEdit = false`\n4. Lock holder can edit; release → third officer can claim\n5. Police cannot list cases; can record police result on pending verification\n6. Mutating handler rejects when locked to another officer\n\nUse existing fake-officer pattern (`ICurrentOfficer` swap in tests).\n\n---\n\n## Documentation\n\nUpdate [docs/features/registration/README.md](docs/features/registration/README.md) and add [docs/phases/phase-8.1-role-boundaries-case-locking.md](docs/phases/phase-8.1-role-boundaries-case-locking.md) with role matrix and lock flow. Bump [docs/ROADMAP.md](docs/ROADMAP.md) progress table.\n\n---\n\n## Out of scope (future)\n\n- `AdminOfficer` role + force-unlock endpoint\n- “Request lock release” notification between colleagues (could reuse `OutboundNotification` stub from Phase 8)\n- Real ASP.NET Identity / policy-based auth (still demo role switcher)\n- Full intake-step audit recording (enum values exist; wire incrementally)\n",
````

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
A helper will be created for mutating handlers to avoid duplicating authorization code.

Mutating handlers will receive ICurrentOfficer and RegistrationCaseAuthorization to enforce permissions before mutations. A domain exception for lock violations may be added. The Open method on RegistrationCase will be updated to accept an optional assignedOfficer.

Claim will succeed only when the case is unassigned or the current lock is free. Releasing a lock clears the holder but preserves the assigned officer. EnsureEditableBy validates the current officer against the lock. IsLockedByOther checks whether another officer holds the lock.

The assigned officer remains after a lock release. Another officer claiming on open reassigns and relocks. TryClaim returns false if already held by the same officer. Otherwise it updates both AssignedOfficerId and LockedByOfficerId.

Reception cases will open unassigned and unlocked. Population cases may auto-claim or remain unassigned, since population assigns on the case detail page rather than during direct opening.

The Open method signature will drop the assigned officer parameter entirely. All existing calls to RegistrationCase.Open in tests will be updated to match.

Existing in-progress cases will be backfilled so their locked_by field matches the assigned_officer_id to preserve current behavior. The assigned_officer_id column will be made nullable in the database. Authorization failures will throw UnauthorizedAccessException, and lock violations will throw a new CaseLockException in the Domain layer.

EnsureEditableBy will throw InvalidRegistrationTransitionException when a case is locked by another officer. Domain tests will cover claim and release behavior. A RegistrationCaseAccess helper will centralize loading editable cases by repository, authorization, and current officer.

The helper will throw KeyNotFoundException for missing cases. It will accept an operation name to pass through to EnsureEditableBy. Alternatively, RegistrationCaseAuthorization may expose a single EnsureCanEdit method that combines role and lock checks before returning the case.

CanEditCase allows population to edit a case only when it is unlocked or already held by that officer. Police handlers will remain separate. List and Get operations will rely solely on role checks. The GetRegistrationCase DTO will compute CanEdit from CanViewCase. ClaimRegistrationCaseHandler will be restricted to population and will return whether it successfully claimed the case. Six mutating handlers need EnsureCanEdit: RecordIdentityHandler, LinkExistingPersonHandler, ConvertBisNumberHandler, CorrectIdentityHandler, SetResidenceCategoryHandler, and RecordResidencePermitHandler.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Beginning implementation of the plan.

Planning test helpers for case claiming. Existing tests will use the default PopulationOfficer. New helpers will open cases then claim them before mutations.

Auto-claiming during case opening was rejected per the plan. Tests will explicitly claim cases after opening before mutations. Auto-claiming on first edit was also dismissed.

A `ClaimCaseAsync` helper will be added for test setup. Existing tests will be updated to claim cases before mutations. There is uncertainty about whether `EnsureEditableBy` should reject edits when a case lacks a lock.

Confirmed that `EnsureEditableBy` rejects edits when a case is unassigned, requiring a claim first. The implementation sequence is domain, migration, authorization, handlers, UI, and tests. The test helper will use Marie as the PopulationOfficer with ID `11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111`.

The CurrentOfficer class will be made mutable by changing OfficerId to a private setter. An Impersonate method will support different IDs per role. Reception will use ID 22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222222. This enables realistic lock contention between multiple population officers.

Police and a second population officer (Anne) were assigned test IDs. SetRole will now set the default OfficerId for each role. The Open method will drop its assignedOfficer parameter. Domain work will be batched across RegistrationCase.cs, CaseAuditAction.cs, RegistrationCaseConfiguration.cs, and the EF migration.
</details></think>

    r&#34;\?\? throw new KeyNotFoundException\(\$\&#34;Registration case &#39;\{caseId\}&#39; was not found\.\&#34;\);&#34;
)

get_pattern2 = re.compile(
    r&#34;var registrationCase = await caseRepository\.GetByIdAsync\(caseId, cancellationToken\);\s*&#34;
    r&#34;if \(registrationCase is null\)\s*\{\s*return null;\s*\}&#34;
)

for path in handlers_dir.rglob(&#34;*Handler.cs&#34;):

```bash
cd /Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality && python3 <<'PY'
import re
from pathlib import Path

handlers_dir = Path("src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration")
skip = {
    "GetRegistrationCaseHandler.cs",
    "ListRegistrationCasesHandler.cs",
    "OpenRegistrationCaseHandler.cs",
    "GetReviewDashboardHandler.cs",
    "ClaimRegistrationCaseHandler.cs",
    "ReleaseCaseLockHandler.cs",
    "ListPendingPoliceVerificationsHandler.cs",
    "ListOutboundNotificationsHandler.cs",
    "SearchNationalRegisterHandler.cs",
    "RecordIdentityHandler.cs",
}

get_pattern = re.compile(
    r"var registrationCase = await caseRepository\.GetByIdAsync\(caseId, cancellationToken\)\s*"
    r"\?\? throw new KeyNotFoundException\(\$\"Registration case '\{caseId\}' was not found\.\"\);"
)

get_pattern2 = re.compile(
    r"var registrationCase = await caseRepository\.GetByIdAsync\(caseId, cancellationToken\);\s*"
    r"if \(registrationCase is null\)\s*\{\s*return null;\s*\}"
)

for path in handlers_dir.rglob("*Handler.cs"):
    if path.name in skip:
        continue
    text = path.read_text()
    if "IRegistrationCaseRepository caseRepository" not in text:
        continue
    if "RegistrationCaseGuard caseGuard" in text:
        continue
    if "GetByIdAsync(caseId" not in text and "GetByIdAsync(\n                verificationRequest.RegistrationCaseId" not in text:
        continue

    text = text.replace(
        "using SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain.Registration;",
        "using SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain.Registration;\nusing SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web.Features.Registration;",
        1,
    )
    text = text.replace(
        "IRegistrationCaseRepository caseRepository,",
        "RegistrationCaseGuard caseGuard,\n    IRegistrationCaseRepository caseRepository,",
        1,
    )

    op = path.stem.replace("Handler", "")
    replacement = f"""var registrationCase = await caseGuard.GetForEditAsync(
            caseId,
            nameof({op}),
            cancellationToken);"""

    new_text, n = get_pattern.subn(replacement, text)
    if n:
        path.write_text(new_text)
        print(f"updated {path}")
    else:
        print(f"skipped (no match) {path}")
PY
```

from pathlib import Path

root = Path(&#34;tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration&#34;)
skip = {&#34;RoleBoundariesAndCaseLockingTests.cs&#34;, &#34;RegistrationTestHelpers.cs&#34;, &#34;CaseDecisionTests.cs&#34;}

pattern = re.compile(
    r&#34;var opened = await openHandler\.Handle\(\s*&#34;

    r&#34;CancellationToken\.None\);\s*&#34;

    re.MULTILINE,
)

replacement = &#34;var caseId = await RegistrationTestHelpers.OpenAndClaimCaseAsync(services if False else scope.ServiceProvider);&#34;

for path in root.glob(&#34;*.cs&#34;):
    if path.name in skip:
        continue
    text = path.read_text()
    if &#34;OpenRegistrationCaseRequest(VisitReason.FirstRegistration, null)&#34; not in text:
        continue

            CancellationToken.None);

        var caseId = new RegistrationCaseId(opened.CaseId);&#34;&#34;&#34;.replace(&#34;services&#34;, &#34;scope.ServiceProvider&#34;),
        &#34;        var caseId = await RegistrationTestHelpers.OpenAndClaimCaseAsync(scope.ServiceProvider);&#34;
    )
    if new_text != text:
        path.write_text(new_text)
        print(&#34;updated&#34;, path.name)

dotnet test --no-build 2&gt;&amp;1 | tail -5</summary>
```bash
cd /Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality && python3 <<'PY'
import re
from pathlib import Path

root = Path("tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration")
skip = {"RoleBoundariesAndCaseLockingTests.cs", "RegistrationTestHelpers.cs", "CaseDecisionTests.cs"}

pattern = re.compile(
    r"var opened = await openHandler\.Handle\(\s*"
    r"new OpenRegistrationCaseRequest\(VisitReason\.FirstRegistration, null\),\s*"
    r"CancellationToken\.None\);\s*"
    r"\s*var caseId = new RegistrationCaseId\(opened\.CaseId\);",
    re.MULTILINE,
)

replacement = "var caseId = await RegistrationTestHelpers.OpenAndClaimCaseAsync(services if False else scope.ServiceProvider);"

for path in root.glob("*.cs"):
    if path.name in skip:
        continue
    text = path.read_text()
    if "OpenRegistrationCaseRequest(VisitReason.FirstRegistration, null)" not in text:
        continue
    # simpler replace for common pattern
    new_text = text.replace(
        """        var opened = await openHandler.Handle(
            new OpenRegistrationCaseRequest(VisitReason.FirstRegistration, null),
            CancellationToken.None);

        var caseId = new RegistrationCaseId(opened.CaseId);""",
        "        var caseId = await RegistrationTestHelpers.OpenAndClaimCaseAsync(services);"
    )
    new_text = new_text.replace(
        """        var opened = await openHandler.Handle(
            new OpenRegistrationCaseRequest(VisitReason.FirstRegistration, null),
            CancellationToken.None);

        var caseId = new RegistrationCaseId(opened.CaseId);""".replace("services", "scope.ServiceProvider"),
        "        var caseId = await RegistrationTestHelpers.OpenAndClaimCaseAsync(scope.ServiceProvider);"
    )
    if new_text != text:
        path.write_text(new_text)
        print("updated", path.name)
PY
dotnet test --no-build 2>&1 | tail -5
```

_**Agent (default)**_

Implemented **Phase 8.1 — Role boundaries, case locking, and activity timeline**. Summary:

### Role boundaries
- **Reception:** Can create cases via `/registration/new-case`; cannot list or open case detail
- **Population:** Full case list/detail; edits only when holding the lock
- **Police:** Police verifications queue only; no case list/detail links

### Case locking
- Cases open **unassigned** (reception creates, population claims on detail open)
- `ClaimRegistrationCase` assigns + locks; `ReleaseCaseLock` clears the lock (assignee kept for history)
- Another population officer gets **read-only** detail with a banner; **Release lock** button for the lock holder

### Activity timeline
Records `CaseOpened`, `CaseAssigned`, `CaseLockReleased`, `PoliceVerificationRequested`, `PoliceResultRecorded`, plus existing decision/certificate events — with readable labels and officer names on the case detail timeline.

### Key files
- Domain: `RegistrationCase.cs`, `CaseAuditAction.cs`
- Auth: `RegistrationCaseAuthorization.cs`, `RegistrationCaseGuard.cs`
- Slices: `ClaimRegistrationCase/`, `ReleaseCaseLock/`
- UI: `NewRegistrationCasePage.razor`, updated `MainLayout.razor`, `RegistrationCaseList.razor`, `RegistrationCaseDetail.razor`
- Migration: `20260705183640_RoleBoundariesAndCaseLocking.cs`
- Tests: `RegistrationCaseLockingTests`, `RoleBoundariesAndCaseLockingTests`
- Docs: [phase-8.1-role-boundaries-case-locking.md](docs/phases/phase-8.1-role-boundaries-case-locking.md)

All **137 tests pass** (59 domain + 78 integration).

**Deferred (as planned):** Admin force-unlock and colleague “request release” notifications.

---

_**User**_

Can we create another Population officer? so we can simulate can locking / unlocking etc

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Planning a clean `DemoOfficerIdentity` approach to support additional population officers for simulating lock and unlock scenarios.

A new `DemoOfficers.cs` file will hold four demo identities. `CurrentOfficer` will gain a `SecondaryPopulationOfficerId` field. `ICurrentOfficer` needs a `SetDemoOfficer` method. The `RoleSwitcher` UI will switch from three roles to four officers using a `MudSelect` with `Guid` values.

The `MudSelect` will iterate over `DemoOfficers.All` to populate its items. `OfficerDisplayNames` will consolidate around this single source of truth. `SetRole` will remain for backward compatibility, defaulting to Marie. Integration tests will shift from `Impersonate` to `SetDemoOfficer` or a dedicated helper for Anne. `CurrentOfficer` will expose `SelectOfficer(Guid id)`.

The `DemoOfficer` record now carries `Id`, `Role`, and `DisplayName`. `DemoOfficers` exposes `Marie`, `Anne`, `Jean`, and `Luc` as static readonly fields. It also provides an `All` read-only list and a `Find` method to look up an officer by GUID.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Adding a second population officer to the role switcher by introducing a shared demo-officers list and wiring the switcher to select by officer identity.

Done. The app bar role switcher now lists **four demo officers** instead of three roles:

| Officer | Role |
|---------|------|
| Jean Martin (Reception) | Reception |
| **Marie Dupont (Population)** | Population |
| **Anne Leroy (Population)** | Population |
| Luc Vermeulen (Police) | Police |

Both Marie and Anne are full population officers (approve, edit when holding the lock, etc.) but have **different officer IDs**, so you can simulate locking between them.

**Try it:**
1. As **Marie** → open a case → it locks to her
2. Switch to **Anne** → same case is read-only (“Locked — Marie Dupont”)
3. Switch back to **Marie** → **Release lock**
4. As **Anne** → open the case again → she claims it

The switcher is in `RoleSwitcher.razor`; officer definitions live in `DemoOfficers.cs`. You may need to reload the app if AppHost was already running when this was added.

_**User**_

So the case is assigned to marie; when I click on Release the lock it doesn't release it (or at least it looks like he does it but then it's reassigned directly) 

---

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Marie cannot release the lock on a case. The system appears to release it, but then immediately reassigns the case.

The root cause is in the RegistrationCaseDetail.razor flow. After ReleaseLockHandler clears the lock, ReloadCase calls ClaimHandler.Handle before GetHandler. This immediately reclaims the case for the current officer.

The fix should prevent ReloadCase from auto-claiming after a manual release. Claiming should only happen on initial page load, not after the user intentionally releases the lock.

Adding a skipClaim parameter to ReloadCase is one possible fix. Another option is to only auto-claim when the case is unassigned or unlocked and the current user does not already hold it. The core problem is that Claim runs on every ReloadCase invocation, including right after release.

Auto-claim should only trigger on the initial page load when the case is unassigned or unlocked. It should be skipped after an explicit release. Claim returns AlreadyHeld if the current user already holds the lock, so reloads after edits remain safe.

The better fix is to claim the case only on the initial page load, not on every reload. Auto-claim should also be restricted to cases where AssignedOfficerId is null, since after release the assigned officer remains assigned while the lock is cleared.

A concrete rule is taking shape. Auto-claim should run only when the current user is a population officer and the case is either unassigned or unlocked. It must be skipped when the case is assigned to the current officer but the lock was just released. This prevents Marie from reclaiming the case after releasing it, while still allowing Anne to claim an unlocked case.

Reconsidering whether Marie should be prevented from auto-claiming on reload after releasing. If she wants to continue working, she may need to click a "Take case" button instead. If she navigates away and back, she should not automatically reclaim the case either. One edge case remains: the case is unlocked but still assigned to her.

The exact auto-claim condition is now clear. Claim should only run when the case is unlocked and either unassigned or not held by the current officer. This prevents Marie from reclaiming the case after releasing it, while still allowing Anne to claim an unlocked case assigned to Marie. Unassigned cases remain claimable by anyone.

The cleanest fix belongs in the UI layer. ReloadCase should check case state before attempting to claim. A chicken-and-egg problem exists: the case state must be known before the claim can be evaluated.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Adding logic to prevent automatic reassignment after a manual release. This could be handled in the handler or the UI.

The approach is to add a `TryAutoClaim` method in the handler. It checks if the case is unlocked before claiming. This prevents automatic reassignment after a manual release.

The auto-claim logic needs two fixes. It should skip claiming when the case is already held by the current officer. It should also skip claiming when the case is locked to another officer. This prevents unnecessary claims after a manual release and avoids snackbar warnings when viewing another officer's case.

A `ShouldAutoClaim` helper will determine when to skip the claim call entirely. The logic skips when the case is already held by the current officer, locked to another, or already assigned to the current officer. It proceeds only when the case is unlocked and unassigned or assigned to someone else. An integration test will verify that a released case does not auto-reclaim. Reloading the case while the lock is held by the current officer will also skip the claim call.

The guard will live inside `ClaimRegistrationCaseHandler.Handle` rather than the UI. When `ShouldAutoClaim` returns false, the handler returns the current state immediately without saving. If the case is already held by the current officer, it returns the existing state. If the case is unlocked but assigned to the current officer, it returns without reclaiming and sets `CanEdit` to false.

Releasing the lock correctly sets `CanEdit` to false, so the user sees a read-only view until they explicitly take the case again. A "Take case" button for unlocked cases was considered but set aside as an optional enhancement.

The integration test will verify that a released case keeps its lock null. The Claim handler will also skip writing audit records on paths where auto-claim is skipped.

Explicit API claims will still work for other officers on unlocked cases. After a manual release, the current holder cannot automatically reclaim the case. A "Take case" button will be added to the UI for population officers when the case is unlocked and not locked by another officer.

The "Take case" button will invoke the claim handler directly. Because the handler would otherwise skip the claim via `ShouldAutoClaim`, an explicit take action must bypass that guard. The chosen approach is to always attempt a claim when the endpoint is called directly, while keeping the auto-claim guard only in the UI path.

The handler will be split into two methods. `TryAutoClaimAsync` will check `ShouldAutoClaim` and return null when the guard fails. `Handle` will always attempt a full claim. The detail page will call `TryAutoClaimAsync` during reloads. The new "Take case" button will call `Handle` directly.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

You were right — that was a bug.

**Cause:** Every `ReloadCase()` (including right after **Release lock**) called `ClaimHandler.Handle`, which immediately re-locked the case to Marie because it was unlocked but still assigned to her.

**Fix:**
- **Auto-claim only when** the case is unassigned, or unlocked and assigned to someone else (so Anne can pick it up when opening the page).
- **Do not auto-claim** when you're the assignee but have released the lock.
- Added an explicit **Take case** button if you want to reclaim it yourself after releasing.

**Updated flow:**
1. **Marie** opens case → locked to Marie  
2. **Release lock** → stays unlocked; list shows “Assigned — Marie Dupont” (not locked)  
3. **Anne** opens the same case → auto-claims and locks to her  
4. Or **Marie** can click **Take case** to lock it again without waiting for Anne  

Reload the app and try the release flow again — it should stick now.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Evaluating where to trigger the page reload during role switches.

MainLayout.OnOfficerChanged will trigger the reload. This handles both same-role officer switches and role changes like Population to Reception. A full reload is needed to reset case detail permissions and clear the current page.

A full browser reload via NavigationManager.NavigateTo with forceLoad is the simplest approach. Child pages do not re-render automatically without navigation.

Reloading the current page would fail for case detail, since it redirects client-side. Instead, navigate to the role-appropriate home page with forceLoad. Each role maps to its default route: Reception to new-case, Population to cases, and Police to police-verifications.

The implementation will use NavigationManager.NavigateTo with forceLoad on the current URI. A helper method checks the current path against the new role and redirects to the appropriate default route if the page is invalid. This handles both cross-role switches and same-role officer changes.

Same-page forceLoad works for same-role switches too. The reload re-initializes Blazor and re-runs lifecycle methods. The helper now returns the same URI when the current page is valid for the new role. Otherwise it redirects to the role-appropriate default route. The call will be placed in MainLayout.OnOfficerChanged after RefreshNavItemsAsync, or directly in RoleSwitcher.
</details></think>

Done. Switching officer in the app bar now triggers a **full page reload** so the new role’s permissions apply immediately.

Behavior:
- **Marie ↔ Anne** on the same case → page reloads; claim/lock state is re-evaluated for the new officer
- **Population → Reception/Police** on a case page → redirected to the right home (`/registration/new-case` or `/registration/police-verifications`)
- **Reception/Police → Population** on their role-specific pages → sent to `/registration/cases`

This is handled in `MainLayout.razor` via `Navigation.NavigateTo(..., forceLoad: true)` when `ICurrentOfficer.Changed` fires. Reload the app to pick up the change.

_**User**_

When I switch from Marie to Anne (officer) it reload the page but the switch is not done I'm still on Marie

---

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Switching from Marie to Anne triggers a page reload, but the selection does not persist. The user remains on Marie.

The selected officer lives only in memory per Blazor circuit. A full reload resets it to the default. Storing the choice in sessionStorage, a cookie, or a URL parameter would persist it across reloads.

A cookie is preferable to sessionStorage for Blazor Server because it survives reloads without JavaScript interop. The switcher would set the cookie on selection, and the CurrentOfficer service would read it on each server request.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Marie is the default officer, so a full reload resets the circuit and loses Anne's selection. Persisting the selected officer across reloads.

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The selected officer will be persisted in a cookie so the switch survives the page reload.

I'll inject `IHttpContextAccessor` into `CurrentOfficer` to read the cookie on construction and write it during `Apply`. This handles the new circuit created by the forceLoad reload.

The cookie will be named `schaerbeek.demo-officer-id`, set to SameSite=Lax, and appended to the response during `Apply`. `AddHttpContextAccessor()` must be registered in the service builder. Existing tests remain unaffected because they bypass the cookie path.

The constructor will read the cookie and apply the saved officer immediately. It uses `ApplyWithoutNotify` to avoid firing a `Changed` event during initialization.

A private `Apply` method will handle the actual state update and cookie write. A boolean `notify` parameter will suppress the `Changed` event during initial load from the cookie. The cookie itself will be marked `IsEssential`.

There is a risk that `forceLoad` may abort the current response before the cookie reaches the browser. The cookie is appended to the response during `Apply`, but the subsequent navigation could prevent it from being saved. This would explain why the switch fails to persist across reloads.

Blazor Server may not reliably persist cookies written during a SignalR circuit response. The cookie approach is therefore unreliable for the full page reload.

Better alternatives include:
- Using sessionStorage via JavaScript interop before forceLoad
- Appending the officer ID to the URL query string
- Using ProtectedBrowserStorage or localStorage

The query string approach avoids JavaScript entirely. The layout will append the selected officer ID to the URL before calling forceLoad. On the next page load, the application will read that query parameter and restore the saved officer. This sidesteps the cookie persistence problem in Blazor Server.

A helper method will append the selected officer ID to the URI query string. If the target URL already contains parameters, it will use an ampersand separator. The query string should be stripped from the current URL before the forced reload to avoid duplicate parameters. On startup, MainLayout will read the query parameter in its initialization and apply the saved officer. The URL can optionally be cleaned afterward.

MainLayout will parse the `demoOfficer` query parameter in `OnInitialized` and call `SelectOfficer` to restore the saved officer. However, `SelectOfficer` fires a `Changed` event that would trigger another reload. A silent selection method is needed to avoid this circular reload.

A `RestoreOfficer` method will silently apply the saved officer during layout initialization without firing `Changed`. The switch flow will use `SelectOfficer` to trigger `Changed` and navigate with the query parameter, while `OnInitialized` will call `RestoreOfficer` to avoid a second reload.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Persisting the selected officer via a query parameter on reload — `forceLoad` creates a new circuit, so in-memory state resets to Marie.

_**Agent (default)**_

That happened because a full reload starts a **new Blazor circuit**, and `CurrentOfficer` always defaulted back to Marie.

**Fix:** the selected officer is now passed in the URL as `?demoOfficer=<guid>` on reload, and restored when the page loads.

Flow when you switch Marie → Anne:
1. Dropdown selects Anne
2. Reload goes to the same page with `?demoOfficer=44444444-...`
3. `MainLayout` reads that param and restores Anne before anything renders

You’ll see Anne in the app bar and top nav after reload. Reload the app and try Marie ↔ Anne again on a case page — the lock/claim state should reflect the correct officer.
